session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8799 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and execute it with the specified PR argument.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll fetch the PR feedback using the specified PR number (8799):

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "No diff available"
Check for file restructuring in the PR

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8799, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8799", "pr_title": "Consolidate documentation from 11 erk-learn plans", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yzjYV", "path": "docs/learned/planning/incremental-dispatch.md", "line": 34, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — source pointer contains inaccurate line number.

Source: `.github/workflows/plan-implement.yml`

Claim: Line 138 is the 'Checkout implementation branch' step.
Reality: The step is at line **148** (verified in the current file).

Risk: Line numbers in YAML workflow files shift with every edit and the reference is already wrong after the previous fix attempt (set to 138 instead of 148).

Suggested fix: Use the step name only, without a line number:
```
<!-- Source: .github/workflows/plan-implement.yml, "Checkout implementation branch" step -->
```
And update the prose to remove the specific line reference.", "created_at": "2026-03-07T21:53:48Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yzjcO", "path": "docs/learned/tui/filter-toggle-pattern.md", "line": 65, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents a specific line number that will change.

Source: `src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py`

Claim: The toggle-all-users help binding is at line 90.
Reality: It is at line **115** (`yield Label("a Toggle all users / my plans", classes="help-binding")`).

Risk: High maintenance burden — line numbers shift as bindings are added/removed.

Suggested fix: Remove the line number and reference by content instead:
> See `src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py` — the label for the 'a' keybinding (Toggle all users / my plans).", "created_at": "2026-03-07T21:53:58Z" }
